2604.20256.title: RADS: Reinforcement Learning-Based Sample Selection Improves Transfer Learning in Low-resource and Imbalanced Clinical Settings
2604.20256.paper_summary: A common strategy in transfer learning is few shot fine-tuning, but its success is highly dependent on the quality of samples selected as training examples. Active learning methods such as uncertainty sampling and diversity sampling can select useful samples. However, under extremely low-resource and class-imbalanced conditions, they often favor outliers rather than truly informative samples, resulting in degraded performance. In this paper, we introduce RADS (Reinforcement Adaptive Domain Sampling), a robust sample selection strategy using reinforcement learning (RL) to identify the most informative samples. Experimental evaluations on several real world clinical datasets show our sample selection strategy enhances model transferability while maintaining robust performance under extreme class imbalance compared to traditional methods.
2604.20256.paper_summary_zh: 一個常見的轉移學習策略是少量樣本的微調，但其成功高度依賴於選擇作為訓練範例的樣本質量。<lb/>主動學習方法，如不確定性採樣和多樣性採樣，可以選擇有用的樣本。<lb/>然而，在極低資源和類別不平衡的條件下，它們往往偏向於異常值，而非真正具有信息量的樣本，導致性能下降。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了RADS（強化自適應領域採樣），這是一種使用強化學習（RL）來識別最具信息量樣本的穩健樣本選擇策略。<lb/>在幾個真實世界的臨床數據集上的實驗評估顯示，我們的樣本選擇策略在保持穩健性能的同時，增強了模型的可轉移性，與傳統方法相比，在極端類別不平衡下表現更佳。
2604.20256.author: Wei Han et.al.
2604.20256.authors: Wei Han, David Martinez, Anna Khanina, Lawrence Cavedon, Karin Verspoor
